task_completion.description: Open the specified FITS image in AstroImageJ
task_completion.visual_evidence: AstroImageJ application window is open and displaying the image 'ngc6652_814w.fits'.

task_completion.description: Perform aperture photometry on the brightest stars
task_completion.visual_evidence: AstroImageJ shows circular photometry apertures drawn on several bright stars in the image, or a measurements table window is open displaying extracted centroid coordinates and flux values.

task_completion.description: Create the bright star catalog text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the file 'bright_star_catalog.txt' being edited or saved in the '~/AstroImages/ngc6652_project/' directory.

task_completion.description: Format the catalog with the required columns and 5 rows
task_completion.visual_evidence: The content of the text file is visible and contains the columns 'Rank', 'X_pixel', 'Y_pixel', and 'Integrated_Flux' (or similar headers), followed by exactly 5 rows of star data.

task_completion.description: Order the stars from brightest to faintest
task_completion.visual_evidence: The rows in the text file are numbered 1 to 5 in the Rank column, and the values in the Integrated_Flux column are in strictly descending order (highest flux at rank 1, lowest at rank 5).

integrity.description: The agent used AstroImageJ to perform the photometry
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the AstroImageJ GUI (image window, measurement tables, aperture tools) rather than relying entirely on terminal commands or Python scripts to extract the data.

integrity.description: The catalog values correspond to actual measurements
integrity.visual_evidence: The coordinates and flux values entered into the text file should match or closely resemble the values seen in the AstroImageJ measurements table.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script to bypass the manual GUI task
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent writing or executing a Python script (e.g., using astropy or photutils) to automatically generate the 'bright_star_catalog.txt' file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target FITS file is a real Hubble Space Telescope WFPC2 observation of the NGC 6652 Globular Cluster using the F814W (I-band) filter. The expected output path is '/home/ga/AstroImages/ngc6652_project/bright_star_catalog.txt'. The scoring script uses a 20-pixel positional tolerance to verify the centroid coordinates.
